title: Client Onboarding Automation for E-Commerce
metaDescription: Done-for-you client onboarding automation for e-commerce stores. New customers and wholesale clients get set up in your voice, on schedule, without you.
excerpt: A done-for-you onboarding sequence that sets up new e-commerce customers and wholesale clients in your voice, on schedule, without the owner doing it by hand.

sections.intro:
In e-commerce, the first 72 hours after a new customer or wholesale client signs up decides whether they stick. You need to confirm the order, send tracking, share care or usage info, ask for the review, and route VIPs differently from one-time buyers. Most online retailers handle this by hand, or with a generic email tool that does not know who the customer is or what they bought.

The AIOS handles new client setup as a standing capability. It watches your store and CRM for new signups and orders, drafts the onboarding sequence in your voice, sends the right messages at the right time, and flags exceptions for a human. You stay in charge. The system just stops the dropped handoffs.
sections.fourCFit:
This page covers a Capability inside the wider AI Operating System. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that produce real artifacts. They only work because the other three layers are already in place:

  • Context: the system knows your brand voice, product catalog, return policy, and which customers count as VIP or wholesale.
  • Connections: it reads from your store, CRM, inbox, and shipping data so every message uses live order information.
  • Capabilities: this onboarding sequence, plus related workflows like review requests and win-back.
  • Cadence: it runs on a schedule, including weekends and holidays, so new buyers do not wait.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this capability is installed, here is what you actually get:

  • A new customer welcome sequence that fires within 5 minutes of the first order, in your voice, with the right product context.
  • A separate wholesale or B2B onboarding track with account setup steps, pricing sheet, and a named contact.
  • Order confirmation, shipping update, and delivery follow-up messages drafted from live order data, not templates with blank fields.
  • A review request that goes out 7 to 14 days after delivery, timed to the product category.
  • A VIP branch for repeat buyers above a spend threshold you set, with a thank-you and an early-access offer.
  • An exception queue: any customer the system is unsure about gets surfaced to you with a one-line summary and a suggested reply.
  • SLA: new signups receive their first message in under 5 minutes, 95 percent of the time.
sections.howWeInstall:
Every AIOS install runs in three phases. No long discovery, no 90-day projects.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at your current onboarding flow, where new customers fall through, and which steps are worth automating first. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we wire the onboarding capability into your store and CRM, write the sequence in your voice, set the VIP and wholesale branches, and test against real recent orders before going live.
  3. Operate (ongoing): we ship one new automation per week, monitor the sequence, and refine the copy based on open rates, reply rates, and review conversions. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
Grounded ranges from e-commerce installs of this capability:

  • Owner time on onboarding and post-purchase messaging drops from 6 to 12 hours per week to under 1 hour of review.
  • First-response time on new orders moves from hours or days to under 5 minutes.
  • Review request conversion typically rises 15 to 35 percent versus a generic post-purchase email.
  • Repeat purchase rate inside 90 days improves 5 to 12 percent once VIP branching is on.
  • Standing guarantee: across your full AIOS, you recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will the messages still sound like us?

Yes. We capture your voice during install using your real past emails and product pages. Every draft is reviewed against that voice profile before it goes out, and you can edit the library any time.

What happens with refunds, complaints, or weird edge cases?

Those never get an automated reply. The system routes them to you with a short summary and the customer history attached, so you can respond as a human in one or two minutes.

Do we have to switch stores or email tools?

No. The AIOS is tool-agnostic. It connects to the store, CRM, and inbox you already use. If something is genuinely holding you back, we will say so, but switching is rarely needed.
